assertions.title: totus metrics get returns data

steps.action: Run: TOTUS_API_KEY=... bun run packages/cli/src/index.ts metrics get sleep_score --from 2026-01-01 --to 2026-01-31 --server-url http://localhost:3000/api
steps.observed: API error (500): An unexpected error occurred

steps.action: Retry with date range matching actual data (2026-03-01 to 2026-03-09)
steps.observed: Same 500 error

steps.action: Checked server logs for error details
steps.observed: Error: Unsupported state or unable to authenticate data at LocalEncryptionProvider.decryptDek — ENCRYPTION_KEY mismatch with seed data

steps.action: Verified via session-based auth (cookie) to isolate: curl with __session cookie also returns 500
steps.expected: Session auth should work if issue is API-key-specific
steps.observed: Same 500 error via session auth — confirms encryption key mismatch is infrastructure-wide, not auth-related

steps.action: Verified code fix: health-data/route.ts uses getResolvedContext() (RC-1 fix applied)
steps.observed: Confirmed: line 135 calls await getResolvedContext(request)

steps.action: Verified data exists: SELECT from health_data_daily WHERE user_id='mock_test_totus_dev' AND metric_type='sleep_score'

terminal: CLI output: ✗ Error: API error (500): An unexpected error occurred
Server log: Error: Unsupported state or unable to authenticate data at LocalEncryptionProvider.decryptDek (src/lib/encryption/index.ts:214:67)
DB verification: 90 sleep_score rows for mock_test_totus_dev, date range 2025-12-10 to 2026-03-09
Code verification: health-data/route.ts line 135 uses getResolvedContext(request)

assertions.issues: ENCRYPTION_KEY in .env.local does not match the key used to encrypt the seed data in health_data_daily. This is an infrastructure issue affecting ALL data decryption (both API key and session auth). The RC-1 code fix (getResolvedContext) IS correctly applied in the health-data route. Re-seeding the database with `bun run db:seed` from apps/web should fix this, but that would modify shared data.

steps.action: Run: bun run packages/cli/src/index.ts shares create --help
steps.observed: Help shows --label, --metrics, --start, --end, --expires, --note flags

steps.action: Run: TOTUS_API_KEY=tot_live_Nbt1Dqpq_... bun run packages/cli/src/index.ts shares create --label 'CLI test share' --metrics sleep_score,hrv --start 2026-01-01 --end 2026-01-31 --expires 7 --note 'Test share from CLI' --server-url http://localhost:3000/api
steps.expected: Share created with correct field names (allowed_metrics, data_start, data_end) and share URL output
steps.observed: Share created successfully. Response includes id, token, share_url, allowed_metrics: [sleep_score, hrv], data_start: 2026-01-01, data_end: 2026-01-31, grant_expires, note, created_at

assertions.title: totus connections sync

steps.action: Run: totus connections list (to check prerequisite)
steps.observed: {"connections": []} — empty list

steps.action: Verified DB: SELECT from provider_connections WHERE user_id='mock_test_totus_dev'
steps.observed: 1 row: id=4c260e95-..., provider=oura, status=active — connection exists

steps.action: Checked connections list route: GET /api/connections (route.ts)
steps.observed: Route still uses getRequestContext() — NOT fixed by RC-1

steps.action: Run: totus connections sync --all
steps.observed: No active connections to sync. (because list returns empty)

steps.action: Run: totus connections sync 4c260e95-0aa9-46e2-9e59-d4fc7472f120 (direct UUID)
steps.observed: Sync succeeded: {"sync_id":"sync_1773259940821","status":"queued","message":"Sync dispatched for oura connection"} — works because CLI falls back to using UUID as route param, and sync route uses getResolvedContext()

terminal: sync --all output: No active connections to sync.
sync <uuid> output: {"sync_id":"sync_1773259940821","status":"queued","message":"Sync dispatched for oura connection"}
DB: provider_connections has 1 active Oura connection for mock_test_totus_dev
Code: apps/web/src/app/api/connections/route.ts line 17 imports getRequestContext, line 22 calls getRequestContext(request)

assertions.issues: GET /api/connections (route.ts) still uses getRequestContext() instead of getResolvedContext(). This causes connections list to return empty for API key auth (userId resolves to __api_key_pending__). The sync route ([provider]/sync/route.ts) was correctly fixed to use getResolvedContext(), but the list route was missed. This breaks 'sync --all' which depends on the list endpoint. Direct sync by UUID works as a workaround but is not the normal user flow.

assertions.title: totus preferences list/set/delete

steps.action: Run: totus preferences list
steps.expected: List of preferences (may be empty initially)
steps.observed: {"preferences": []} — empty, which is expected initially

steps.action: Run: totus preferences set hrv oura
steps.observed: {"metric_type":"hrv","provider":"oura","updated_at":"2026-03-11T20:13:01.336Z"} — success

steps.action: Verify in DB: SELECT from metric_source_preferences WHERE user_id='mock_test_totus_dev'
steps.observed: 1 row: user_id=mock_test_totus_dev, metric_type=hrv, provider=oura — correctly stored

steps.action: Run: totus preferences list (after set)
steps.observed: {"preferences": []} — STILL EMPTY despite preference existing in DB

steps.action: Checked list route: GET /api/metric-preferences (route.ts)
steps.observed: Route still uses getRequestContext() — NOT fixed by RC-1

steps.action: Run: totus preferences delete hrv
steps.observed: {"metric_type":"hrv","preference":"cleared"} — success

steps.action: Verify in DB: SELECT from metric_source_preferences WHERE user_id='mock_test_totus_dev'
steps.observed: 0 rows — correctly deleted

terminal: preferences list (after set) output: {"preferences": []} — still empty
DB verification confirms set stored data correctly and delete removed it
Code: apps/web/src/app/api/metric-preferences/route.ts line 14 imports getRequestContext, line 19 calls getRequestContext(request)

assertions.issues: GET /api/metric-preferences (route.ts) still uses getRequestContext() instead of getResolvedContext(). This causes preferences list to return empty for API key auth. The PUT and DELETE routes ([metricType]/route.ts) were correctly fixed to use getResolvedContext(), but the GET list route was missed. Set and delete operations work correctly, but list always returns empty.

frictions.description: CLI output is JSON format because terminal is non-TTY (piped). This means table format assertions can't be verified through this testing method.
frictions.resolution: Accepted JSON output as equivalent evidence since the data content is correct

blockers.description: ENCRYPTION_KEY mismatch: the current ENCRYPTION_KEY in .env.local does not match the key used to encrypt seed data in health_data_daily. All decrypt operations fail with 'Unsupported state or unable to authenticate data' at LocalEncryptionProvider.decryptDek. This affects ALL users (session and API key auth) and ALL routes that decrypt health data.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried with different date ranges, verified with session-based auth (same failure). Confirmed data exists in DB (720 rows for mock_test_totus_dev). Re-seeding would fix but would modify shared data.

blockers.description: RC-1 fix incomplete: getRequestContext() was NOT changed to getResolvedContext() in two list routes: GET /api/connections (apps/web/src/app/api/connections/route.ts) and GET /api/metric-preferences (apps/web/src/app/api/metric-preferences/route.ts). These routes still resolve API key auth to userId='__api_key_pending__' instead of the real user ID.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified the routes' source code. The fix was applied to sync and set/delete routes but the list routes were missed.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-CLI-009), 2 failed (VAL-CLI-013, VAL-CLI-014), 1 blocked (VAL-CLI-007). VAL-CLI-009 passes — RC-3 fix confirmed, shares create sends correct field names. VAL-CLI-013 and VAL-CLI-014 fail because the RC-1 fix was incomplete: GET /api/connections and GET /api/metric-preferences still use getRequestContext() instead of getResolvedContext(), causing list operations to return empty for API key auth. VAL-CLI-007 is blocked by an infrastructure issue: ENCRYPTION_KEY mismatch prevents health data decryption (the RC-1 code fix IS correctly applied in the health-data route).
